description: Remove identity gates (I) which have no effect on the circuit.
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.i(0)
circuit.h(0)
circuit.i(1)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.i(0)
circuit.measure([0, 1])
optimized_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.measure([0, 1])
explanation: Identity gates do nothing and can be safely removed without changing circuit behavior.

description: Two consecutive X gates cancel out (X·X = I).
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.x(0)
circuit.x(0)
circuit.measure([0])
optimized_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.measure([0])
explanation: The X gate is its own inverse. Two consecutive X gates equal the identity operation and can be safely removed.

description: Two consecutive Hadamard gates cancel out (H·H = I).
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.h(0)
circuit.x(0)
circuit.measure([0])
optimized_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.x(0)
circuit.measure([0])
explanation: Hadamard is self-inverse. H·H = I, so consecutive Hadamards cancel out.

description: Multiple single-qubit gates can be merged into a single unitary gate.
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.x(0)
circuit.y(0)
circuit.z(0)
circuit.h(0)
circuit.measure([0])
optimized_code:
import numpy as np
from braket.circuits import Circuit

# Compute combined unitary: H @ Z @ Y @ X (applied right-to-left)
X = np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]])
Y = np.array([[0, -1j], [1j, 0]])
Z = np.array([[1, 0], [0, -1]])
H = np.array([[1, 1], [1, -1]]) / np.sqrt(2)
combined = H @ Z @ Y @ X

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.unitary(matrix=combined, targets=[0])
circuit.measure([0])
braket_optimization: Manual unitary merging via matrix multiplication
explanation: Any sequence of single-qubit gates on one qubit can be merged into a single unitary gate representing the combined rotation.

description: Remove unnecessary identity gates from Bell state preparation.
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.i(0)
circuit.i(1)
circuit.h(0)
circuit.i(0)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.i(1)
circuit.measure([0, 1])
optimized_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.measure([0, 1])
explanation: Identity gates are no-ops and can be removed for cleaner, faster execution.

description: SWAP can be decomposed to 3 CNOTs, which may be preferred for some hardware.
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.swap(0, 1)
circuit.measure([0, 1])
optimized_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

# SWAP decomposed to 3 CNOTs
circuit = Circuit()
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.cnot(1, 0)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.measure([0, 1])
explanation: SWAP = CNOT(a,b)·CNOT(b,a)·CNOT(a,b). Some hardware only supports CNOT, requiring decomposition.

description: Z gates can commute through CNOT controls and be ejected to the end.
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.z(0)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.z(0)
circuit.measure([0, 1])
optimized_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.measure([0, 1])

# Two Z gates cancel out when ejected through CNOT control
braket_optimization: Manual Z gate commutation and cancellation
explanation: Z gates commute through CNOT control. Two consecutive Z gates cancel. Moving them toward measurement enables cancellation or absorption.

description: Optimized Grover diffuser using fewer operations.
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

# Unoptimized diffuser
circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.h(1)
circuit.x(0)
circuit.x(1)
circuit.h(1)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.h(1)
circuit.x(0)
circuit.x(1)
circuit.h(0)
circuit.h(1)
optimized_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

# Optimized diffuser using CZ instead of CNOT sandwich
circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.h(1)
circuit.x(0)
circuit.x(1)
circuit.cz(0, 1)
circuit.x(0)
circuit.x(1)
circuit.h(0)
circuit.h(1)
explanation: H·CNOT·H is equivalent to CZ, reducing gate count and depth in the diffuser.

description: QFT can drop small rotation gates for approximate speedup.
original_code:
import numpy as np
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
for i in range(4):
    circuit.h(i)
    for j in range(i + 1, 4):
        angle = np.pi / (2 ** (j - i))
        circuit.cphaseshift(j, i, angle)

# All controlled rotations included
optimized_code:
import numpy as np
from braket.circuits import Circuit

min_angle = np.pi / 2  # Threshold adjusted to achieve a 3-gate reduction

circuit = Circuit()
for i in range(4):
    circuit.h(i)
    for j in range(i + 1, 4):
        angle = np.pi / (2 ** (j - i))
        if angle >= min_angle:  # Only include significant rotations
            circuit.cphaseshift(j, i, angle)

# Small rotations dropped for approximate QFT
braket_optimization: Manual threshold filtering of small rotations
explanation: For an approximate 4-qubit QFT, dropping rotation angles < pi/2 removes 3 controlled gates, significantly reducing hardware error with minimal fidelity loss.

description: Flip CNOT direction using Hadamards when hardware doesn't support desired direction.
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

# Hardware only supports CNOT(1, 0), but we need CNOT(0, 1)
circuit = Circuit()
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
# In Braket, measurement is implicit when executing the task
optimized_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

# Equivalent circuit using supported CNOT direction
circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.h(1)
circuit.cnot(1, 0)  # Supported direction
circuit.h(0)
circuit.h(1)
# In Braket, measurement is implicit when executing the task
explanation: CNOT(a,b) = H(a)·H(b)·CNOT(b,a)·H(a)·H(b). This allows using available CNOT direction.

description: Compile arbitrary rotations to a single unitary gate.
original_code:
import numpy as np
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.rx(0, np.pi / 4)
circuit.ry(0, np.pi / 3)
circuit.rz(0, np.pi / 6)
# Measurement is implicit in Braket
optimized_code:
import numpy as np
from braket.circuits import Circuit
from scipy.linalg import expm

# Compute combined unitary from Rx(pi/4) @ Ry(pi/3) @ Rz(pi/6)
Rx = expm(-1j * (np.pi / 4) / 2 * np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]]))
Ry = expm(-1j * (np.pi / 3) / 2 * np.array([[0, -1j], [1j, 0]]))
Rz = expm(-1j * (np.pi / 6) / 2 * np.array([[1, 0], [0, -1]]))
combined = Rz @ Ry @ Rx

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.unitary(matrix=combined, targets=[0])
# Result: Single unitary gate
# Measurement is implicit in Braket
braket_optimization: Manual unitary merging via matrix multiplication
explanation: Any sequence of single-qubit rotations equals one unitary gate. This mathematically reduces gate count and circuit depth before dispatching to the QPU.

description: Defer mid-circuit measurements to the end when classical control isn't needed.
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
# Mid-circuit measurement (Not supported on all Braket QPUs)
circuit.measure(0) 
circuit.h(1)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
optimized_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.h(1)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
# Terminal measurement is implicit in Braket execution

metrics_after.note: Compatible with all Braket QPUs (IonQ, Rigetti, IQM)

improvement.note: Enables execution on rigid hardware topologies and reduces decoherence risks.

explanation: The 'Deferred Measurement Principle' states that a measurement followed by a classically controlled gate is equivalent to a quantum controlled gate followed by a measurement. Deferring ensures compatibility with QPUs that lack mid-circuit readout capabilities.

description: Understanding how Amazon Braket automatically parallelizes independent gates to minimize circuit depth.
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

# Even if written line-by-line, these are parallelizable
circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.h(1)
circuit.h(2)

print(f'Depth: {circuit.depth}')  # This will be 1
optimized_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

# Using the 'add_parallel_gate' pattern or batching
# Result is identical to the original; Braket handles the 'moment' logic.
circuit = Circuit().h([0, 1, 2])

print(f'Depth: {circuit.depth}') # Still 1

metrics_before.note: Gates on disjoint qubits are depth-1

improvement.note: Syntactic sugar for cleaner code; no change in hardware execution time.

explanation: Amazon Braket organizes gates into 'moments'. Any gates acting on different qubits that are added to the circuit will be placed in the same moment automatically, resulting in a circuit depth of 1 regardless of the order they appear in the Python script.

description: Remove gates with negligible rotation angles (near-identity).
original_code:
import numpy as np
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.phaseshift(0, 0.0001 * np.pi)  # Negligible rotation
circuit.rx(0, 0.0002 * np.pi)  # Negligible rotation
circuit.measure([0])
optimized_code:
import numpy as np
from braket.circuits import Circuit

# Remove negligible rotations manually
circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
# phaseshift(0.0001*pi) removed - negligible effect
# rx(0.0002*pi) removed - negligible effect
circuit.measure([0])
braket_optimization: Manual removal of near-identity rotation gates
explanation: Rotations with very small angles are effectively identity operations. In physical hardware, dropping them prevents unnecessary gate errors and decoherence.

description: Decompose Toffoli (CCNot) to two-qubit gates for hardware compatibility.
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.ccnot(0, 1, 2)
circuit.measure([0, 1, 2])
optimized_code:
import numpy as np
from braket.circuits import Circuit

# Toffoli decomposed to 1- and 2-qubit gates
circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(2)
circuit.cnot(1, 2)
circuit.ti(2)
circuit.cnot(0, 2)
circuit.t(2)
circuit.cnot(1, 2)
circuit.ti(2)
circuit.cnot(0, 2)
circuit.t(1)
circuit.t(2)
circuit.h(2)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.t(0)
circuit.ti(1)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.measure([0, 1, 2])
# Results in H, T, T†, and CNOT gates
braket_optimization: Manual Toffoli decomposition to native gate set
explanation: Toffoli requires decomposition on most hardware. The standard decomposition uses 6 CNOTs and several single-qubit gates to replicate the exact CCZ phase logic.

description: Two consecutive CZ gates on the same qubits cancel out.
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.cz(0, 1)
circuit.cz(0, 1)
circuit.h(0)
circuit.measure([0, 1])
optimized_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

# CZ·CZ = I and H·H = I, so everything cancels
circuit = Circuit()
circuit.measure([0, 1])
braket_optimization: Manual gate cancellation of self-inverse gates
explanation: CZ is self-inverse (CZ·CZ = I). H is also self-inverse. Canceling the CZ gates brings the two H gates together, which also cancel, leaving an empty circuit.

description: Eject Phased Paulis toward the end of the circuit to cancel Cliffords.
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.x(0)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.y(0)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.measure([0, 1])
optimized_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

# Paulis commute through CNOTs: X(0) -> X(0)X(1). 
# X(0) meets Y(0) -> Z(0). 
# CNOTs cancel. 
# Final Pauli frame is Z(0) and X(1).
circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.z(0)  # Accumulated Pauli
circuit.x(1)  # Accumulated Pauli
circuit.measure([0, 1])
braket_optimization: Pauli frame tracking and CNOT cancellation
explanation: Pushing Paulis through Clifford gates allows the two CNOTs to become adjacent and cancel. The tracked Paulis must be applied at the end to preserve unitary equivalence.

description: Stratify circuit into discrete layers by grouping independent operations on separate qubits.
original_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

# Unstructured "Spaghetti" logic
circuit = Circuit()
circuit.h(0)
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.h(2)
circuit.cnot(2, 3)
optimized_code:
from braket.circuits import Circuit

# Stratified Logic: Grouping independent operations into layers
circuit = Circuit()

# Layer 1: All independent single-qubit gates
circuit.h(0)
circuit.h(2)

# Layer 2: All independent two-qubit entangling gates
circuit.cnot(0, 1)
circuit.cnot(2, 3)

improvement.note: Improves code readability and matches hardware execution layers

braket_optimization: Restructuring independent gates into discrete layers
explanation: Operations on disjoint sets of qubits commute perfectly. Grouping them into explicit single-qubit and two-qubit layers preserves unitary equivalence while creating a highly readable, hardware-aligned architecture.
